rationale: The citing paper’s core analysis is a meta-analysis of brand-level selective-demand elasticities with a study database ending in 2008, while the cited paper (2010) focuses on primary-demand decomposition in pharmaceuticals, so it is unlikely to be a direct input to the main meta-analytic estimates. However, the pair has topical overlap (DTC/pharma advertising) and the only match evidence is a single DOI reference with no local citation context. That makes it unclear whether this is just background contrast or a substantive conceptual/method reference.
